summary: Implemented deterministic M4 daily march execution, march supply state, reinforcement join/failure/synchronization timing, protocol command/query parsing, and scenario/invariant coverage. The implementation reuses existing M4 campaign objective, muster commitment, grain reservation, and M2 route-capacity preview abstractions; no siege, battle, governance, campaign AI policy, client UI, save-format, or content systems were changed.

tests_run.result: ESLint passed for configured app, package, test, storybook, and tools source globs.
Node and worker-compatible deterministic hello, command/query, and save/load simulations matched.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; 30 root Vitest files and 198 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file and 3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-MARCH-SUPPLY-STATE-001

decisions: Added shared sim.start-campaign-march GameCommand and sim.list-m4-march-state GameQuery so player and AI use the same protocol path.
Daily march execution runs inside sim.advance-day after scheduler day advancement and restamps the same revision/hash instead of committing a second authoritative revision.
March start transfers existing M4 carried grain reservations into authoritative march supply state without creating new resources.
Late reinforcements join once through existing mobilized commitments; late joins create a one-day reinforcement synchronization delay before further movement.
Rainy-season monsoon risk pauses march progress before supply consumption for that day.
risks: R3 scope: protocol and authoritative WorldState schema surface changed; systems_architect review requested before acceptance.
M4 march state is added to live world state but save-format DTOs were intentionally not modified per task boundary; reviewer should confirm whether save compatibility follow-up is needed.
requested_action: Review deterministic M4 march execution, protocol additions, WorldState march state invariants, and the scenario/property tests. Do not mark ACCEPTED until architecture review passes.
